annotations.label: Z-transform

anchor.snippet: the Z-transform converts a discrete-time signal

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Z-transform definition or signal-processing theory.

annotations.label: Bilateral Z-transform

anchor.snippet: The bilateral or two-sided Z-transform of a discrete-time signal

annotations.note: No bilateral Z-transform appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polar form of z

anchor.snippet: In polar form

annotations.note: Mathlib has polar coordinates for complex numbers, but not in a Z-transform context.

annotations.label: Unilateral Z-transform

anchor.snippet: the single-sided or unilateral Z-transform is defined as

annotations.note: No unilateral Z-transform appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Probability-generating function

anchor.snippet: An important example of the unilateral Z-transform is the probability-generating function

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the moment generating function (`ProbabilityTheory.mgf`) but no probability generating function for integer-valued RVs.

annotations.label: Inverse Z-transform

anchor.snippet: The inverse Z-transform is

annotations.note: No inverse Z-transform contour integral formula in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inverse Z-transform on unit circle

anchor.snippet: A special case of this contour integral occurs when

annotations.note: Unit-circle inversion formula relating Z-transform to Fourier series not formalized.

annotations.label: Direct evaluation by contour integration

anchor.snippet: This method involves applying the Cauchy Residue Theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a general Cauchy residue theorem applicable to Z-transform inversion.

annotations.label: Series expansion method

anchor.section: Expansion into a series of terms in the variables z and z −1
anchor.snippet: the Z-transform is expanded into a power series

annotations.note: Z-transform series-expansion inversion method is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Partial-fraction expansion method

anchor.snippet: This technique decomposes the Z-transform into a sum of simpler fractions

annotations.note: No partial-fraction-based Z-transform inversion in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inverse Z-transform by series expansion

anchor.snippet: Determine the inverse Z-transform of the following by series expansion method

annotations.note: Specific Z-transform inversion examples are not formalized.

annotations.label: Region of convergence

anchor.snippet: The region of convergence (ROC) is the set of points in the complex plane

annotations.note: ROC of a Z-transform is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Example 1 (no ROC)

anchor.snippet: Therefore, there are no values of

annotations.note: Z-transform ROC example not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Example 2 (causal ROC)

anchor.snippet: The last equality arises from the infinite geometric series

annotations.note: Causal ROC example not formalized; only the underlying geometric series sum (`tsum_geometric_of_norm_lt_one`) exists.

annotations.label: Example 3 (anticausal ROC)

anchor.snippet: using the infinite geometric series again

annotations.note: Anticausal ROC example not formalized.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of Z-transform with ROC

anchor.snippet: is unique when and only when specifying the ROC

annotations.note: Uniqueness of Z-transform together with ROC is not formalized.

annotations.label: ROC excludes poles

anchor.snippet: the ROC will never contain poles

annotations.note: No ROC-poles relationship in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stability via ROC

anchor.snippet: The stability of a system can also be determined by knowing the ROC alone

annotations.note: BIBO stability characterization via ROC not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Causality, stability and pole location

anchor.snippet: If we need both stability and causality

annotations.note: Pole-location characterization of causal stable systems not formalized.

anchor.snippet: Parseval's theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib has Parseval for Fourier series on the circle, but not the Z-transform version.

anchor.snippet: Initial value theorem

annotations.note: Z-transform initial value theorem not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Final value theorem

anchor.snippet: Final value theorem

annotations.note: Z-transform final value theorem not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unit step function

anchor.snippet: is the unit (or Heaviside) step function

annotations.note: No Heaviside step function found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Discrete-time unit impulse

anchor.snippet: is the discrete-time unit impulse function

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Pi.single` (or Kronecker delta via `if n = 0 then 1 else 0`) captures the discrete impulse, but not labeled as such for signal processing.

annotations.label: Bilateral transform as Fourier series

anchor.section: Relationship to Fourier series and Fourier transform
anchor.snippet: The bilateral transform reduces to a Fourier series

annotations.note: Connection between Z-transform on unit circle and Fourier series not formalized.

annotations.label: Bilinear transform

anchor.snippet: The bilinear transform can be used to convert continuous-time filters

annotations.note: Bilinear (Tustin) transform between s- and z-planes not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Starred transform

anchor.snippet: the corresponding starred transform produces a Laplace transform

annotations.note: Starred transform not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: LCCD equation

anchor.snippet: The linear constant-coefficient difference (LCCD) equation is a representation for a linear system

annotations.note: LCCD equations not formalized as a structure in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Transfer function

anchor.snippet: Rearranging results in the system's transfer function

annotations.note: No transfer-function concept tied to Z-transform in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zeros and poles via fundamental theorem of algebra

anchor.snippet: From the fundamental theorem of algebra the numerator has

annotations.note: The fundamental theorem of algebra (`Complex.exists_root`, `Complex.isAlgClosed`) is in Mathlib but not its application to transfer-function zeros/poles.

annotations.label: Equal number of zeros and poles

anchor.snippet: the number of zeros and poles are always equal

annotations.note: Equality of zero/pole counts (including at infinity) for rational transfer functions not formalized.

annotations.label: Output response via inverse Z-transform

anchor.snippet: By performing partial fraction decomposition

annotations.note: Output-response computation via inverse Z-transform not formalized.
